Hierarchy is represented explicitly as Module -> Subground -> Topic while authorities remain graph-native.
Neo4j constraints and import templates are included for database-backed deployment.

nodes.label: Dispute Resolution

nodes.label: Agreement (Offer and Acceptance)
nodes.summary: A contract is a legally enforceable agreement, and the existence of an agreement is determined objectively based on what a reasonable person would conclude from the parties' words and actions.

nodes.label: Offer vs. Invitation to Treat
nodes.summary: An offer must be distinguished from an invitation to treat; for example, the display of goods with a price tag or an advertisement is generally merely an invitation to treat, not a binding offer.

nodes.label: Communication of Acceptance
nodes.summary: Acceptance must generally be communicated to the offeror, but under the "postal rule," acceptance by post may be effective upon mailing. For electronic communications, receipt may depend on whether an "information system" was designated under the Electronic Transactions Ordinance (Cap. 553).

nodes.label: Consideration
nodes.summary: A fundamental requirement for all contracts not made under seal is consideration, which must be "sufficient" (of some value in the eyes of the law) but need not be "adequate" (of equal value). Past consideration is generally not valid consideration. However, the performance of an existing contractual duty may constitute valid consideration if it confers a "practical benefit" on the promisor, following the principle in Williams v Roffey Bros.

nodes.label: Intention to Create Legal Relations
nodes.summary: For an agreement to be enforceable, the parties must intend to create legal relations, which is presumed in commercial contexts but generally presumed absent in social or domestic arrangements.
nodes.path: Formation/Intention to Create Legal Relations/Intention to Create Legal Relations

nodes.label: Capacity
nodes.summary: Exceptions to full contractual capacity include minors, drunkards, and those with mental incapacity. Contracts for "necessaries" or beneficial contracts of employment are enforceable against minors, who must pay a reasonable price for them.

nodes.label: Formality
nodes.summary: The general rule is that contracts can be made in any form, including orally. A major exception is contracts for the sale or disposition of land, which must be in writing or evidenced by a signed memorandum under section 3(1) of the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance (Cap. 219), subject to the equitable doctrine of part performance. Guarantees no longer need to be in writing under Hong Kong law.

nodes.label: Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule
nodes.label_en: Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule
nodes.summary: Where parties have reduced their agreement to writing, the parol evidence rule generally prevents the admission of extrinsic or oral evidence to add to, vary, or contradict the written terms.
nodes.path: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

nodes.label: Implied Terms
nodes.summary: Terms may be implied by custom, in law, by statute, or in fact to give "business efficacy" to the contract or because they are so obvious they "go without saying" (the officious bystander test).

nodes.label: Statutory Implied Terms
nodes.summary: The Sale of Goods Ordinance (Cap. 26) implies conditions that the seller has the right to sell, and that goods will correspond with their description, be of merchantable quality, and be fit for purpose. The Supply of Services (Implied Terms) Ordinance (Cap. 457) implies terms that services will be carried out with reasonable care and skill, within a reasonable time, and for a reasonable charge.

nodes.label: Classification of Terms
nodes.summary: Classification of Terms:

nodes.label: Conditions
nodes.summary: Essential terms; any breach entitles the innocent party to terminate the contract and claim damages.

nodes.label: Warranties
nodes.summary: Lesser terms; breach entitles the innocent party only to claim damages, not to terminate.

nodes.label: Innominate (Intermediate) Terms
nodes.summary: The right to terminate depends on whether the consequences of the breach deprive the innocent party of substantially the whole benefit of the contract.

nodes.label: Exemption Clauses
nodes.summary: Clauses attempting to exclude or restrict liability are construed strictly against the party relying on them (the contra proferentem rule).

nodes.label: Statutory Control of Exemption Clauses
nodes.summary: Under the Control of Exemption Clauses Ordinance (Cap. 71), a party cannot exclude liability for death or personal injury resulting from negligence. Excluding other negligence liability, or liability for breach of contract when dealing on standard terms or with a consumer, is only valid if the clause satisfies the statutory test of "reasonableness".
nodes.path: Contents/Exemption Clauses/Statutory Control of Exemption Clauses

nodes.label: Misrepresentation
nodes.summary: A false statement of fact that induces a party to enter into a contract renders the contract voidable. Remedies include rescission and damages under the Misrepresentation Ordinance (Cap. 284), which allows damages for negligent misrepresentation unless the representor proves they had reasonable grounds to believe the statement was true.

nodes.label: Mistake
nodes.summary: A contract may be void if it is affected by an operative mistake, including common mistake (both parties make the same fundamental mistake), mutual mistake (parties are at cross-purposes), or unilateral mistake (one party is mistaken as to the terms or identity, and the other knows it).

nodes.label: Duress
nodes.summary: Illegitimate pressure, including economic duress, that coerces the will of a party and leaves them with no practical alternative makes the contract voidable.

nodes.label: Undue Influence
nodes.summary: An equitable doctrine rendering a contract voidable where consent is obtained through unacceptable influence; it can be actual or presumed from a relationship of trust and confidence (e.g., parent/child, solicitor/client) where the transaction calls for an explanation.

nodes.label: Unconscionability
nodes.summary: The Unconscionable Contracts Ordinance (Cap. 458) empowers courts to refuse to enforce, or to revise, unconscionable terms in contracts where one party deals as a consumer for goods or services. At common law, unconscionability applies sparingly where a party in a superior bargaining position abuses that position to produce an agreement with severe unfairness.

nodes.label: Illegality
nodes.summary: Contracts that are illegal as formed (e.g., agreements to commit a crime, or unauthorized gambling under the Gambling Ordinance) or contrary to public policy are generally void and unenforceable.

nodes.label: By Performance
nodes.summary: The general rule requires exact and precise performance of contractual obligations.

nodes.label: By Breach
nodes.summary: A repudiatory breach, or a breach of a condition, gives the innocent party an "election": they may choose to affirm the contract or accept the repudiation to terminate the contract and claim damages.

nodes.label: By Frustration
nodes.summary: Occurs when a supervening, unforeseeable event, occurring without the fault of either party, makes performance impossible or radically transforms the nature of the contractual obligations. The Law Amendment and Reform (Consolidation) Ordinance (Cap. 23) grants courts discretion to adjust the financial rights and liabilities of the parties following frustration.

nodes.label: Unliquidated Damages
nodes.summary: The primary common law remedy. The aim is compensatory: to put the innocent party in the position they would have been in had the contract been properly performed.

nodes.label: Remoteness of Damage
nodes.summary: Under the rule in Hadley v Baxendale, losses are only recoverable if they arise naturally in the ordinary course of things, or were reasonably foreseeable as a probable result of the breach at the time the contract was made.

nodes.label: Liquidated Damages vs. Penalties
nodes.summary: A liquidated damages clause specifying a sum payable upon breach is enforceable if it is a genuine pre-estimate of loss or protects a legitimate interest proportionally; if it is out of all proportion and penal in nature, it is an unenforceable penalty.

nodes.label: Equitable Remedies
nodes.summary: Specific performance (an order to fulfill the contract) and injunctions (an order to refrain from breaching a negative obligation) are discretionary and generally only granted when monetary damages are inadequate.

nodes.label: Derived Topics
nodes.summary: Synthetic subground created to absorb additional graph concepts under Cross-Cutting Issues.

nodes.label: Restitution (Unjust Enrichment)
nodes.summary: Available exceptionally, such as when there has been a "total failure of basis" or consideration, allowing a party to recover money paid under a void or discharged contract.

nodes.label: Statutory Exceptions
nodes.summary: The Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Ordinance (Cap. 623) provides a major exception, allowing a third party to enforce a contractual term if the contract expressly provides that they may do so, or if the term purports to confer a benefit on the third party, provided the third party is expressly identified.

nodes.label: Sale of Goods and Services
nodes.summary: Contracts are governed by the Sale of Goods Ordinance (Cap. 26) and the Supply of Services (Implied Terms) Ordinance (Cap. 457), which imply strict conditions regarding title, description, merchantable quality, and reasonable care and skill. Key sub-topics include the passing of property/title, the nemo dat rule (a seller cannot pass better title than they have), and retention of title (Romalpa) clauses.
nodes.path: Special Contracts/Sale of Goods/Sale of Goods and Services

nodes.label: Sale of Land and Leases
nodes.summary: Contracts for the sale or disposition of land must be evidenced in writing under section 3(1) of the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance (Cap. 219), subject to the equitable doctrine of part performance. The framework should map Provisional Agreements for Sale and Purchase (PASP)—which are legally binding—and the vendor's implied duty to prove and give "good title".
nodes.path: Special Contracts/Sale of Land/Sale of Land and Leases

nodes.label: Employment Contracts
nodes.summary: A critical distinction must be drawn between a contract of service (employer-employee, attracting vicarious liability and statutory protections) and a contract for services (independent contractor). These contracts feature an implied duty of mutual trust and confidence.

nodes.label: Construction Contracts
nodes.summary: These heavily rely on standard forms (e.g., HKIA, FIDIC, and Government forms). Key nodes include variations, certification by architects, performance bonds, and "retention" monies held until practical completion.

nodes.label: Credit, Security, and Guarantees
nodes.summary: Differentiate between mortgages, charges (fixed and floating), pledges, and contractual liens. Link the Money Lenders Ordinance (Cap. 163), which polices extortionate interest rates and requires strict formalities for loan agreements. Also, map Suretyship (Guarantees vs. Indemnities) and "on demand" bonds.
nodes.path: Special Contracts/Credit, Security, and Guarantees/Credit, Security, and Guarantees

nodes.label: Insurance Contracts
nodes.summary: Governed by the stringent duty of utmost good faith (uberrimae fidei), requiring full disclosure of material facts. The insured must have an "insurable interest" in the subject matter, distinguishing it from wagering contracts.

nodes.label: Electronic Contracting
nodes.summary: Map how offer and acceptance apply to clickwrap and browsewrap agreements. The Electronic Transactions Ordinance (Cap. 553) validates electronic records and electronic/digital signatures for contract formation.
nodes.path: Cross-Cutting Issues/Electronic Contracting and Emerging Technology/Electronic Contracting

nodes.label: Smart Contracts & Blockchain
nodes.summary: Define smart contracts as "agreements in digital form that are self-executing and self-enforcing". The graph should account for the legal characterization of digital tokens (e.g., as virtual commodities, securities, or choses in action/property).
nodes.path: Cross-Cutting Issues/Electronic Contracting and Emerging Technology/Smart Contracts & Blockchain

nodes.label: Data Privacy in Contracts
nodes.summary: The Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (Cap. 486) interacts heavily with cloud computing and IT outsourcing contracts, requiring data users to bind third-party processors contractually to safeguard data.
nodes.path: Cross-Cutting Issues/Electronic Contracting and Emerging Technology/Data Privacy in Contracts

nodes.label: Choice of Law
nodes.summary: If parties make an express agreement on governing law, it is generally respected. Without it, courts seek an implied choice or apply the system of law with the "closest and most real connection" to the transaction (evaluating place of performance, residence, etc.).

nodes.label: Long-Arm Jurisdiction
nodes.summary: Under RHC O.11, Hong Kong courts can take jurisdiction over overseas defendants if the contract was made in HK, governed by HK law, or breached in HK, subject to forum non conveniens discretion.

nodes.label: Foreign Illegality
nodes.summary: A contract may be unenforceable in Hong Kong if its performance requires violating the laws of a friendly foreign state, based on international comity.

nodes.label: Arbitration
nodes.summary: Map the Arbitration Ordinance (Cap. 609), which enforces valid arbitration agreements and excludes court jurisdiction. The tribunal has the power to rule on its own jurisdiction (kompetenz-kompetenz).

nodes.label: Mediation
nodes.summary: Governed by the Mediation Ordinance (Cap. 620), which protects the strict confidentiality of mediation communications. Mention hybrid models like "Arb-Med".

nodes.label: While not strictly contract law, this is the
nodes.label_en: While not strictly contract law, this is the
nodes.summary: While not strictly contract law, this is the vital fallback when a contract is void, frustrated, or rescinded. The cause of action requires showing that: (1) the defendant was enriched, (2) it was at the plaintiff's expense, and (3) there is an unjust factor (such as mistake, duress, or a total failure of consideration).

nodes.case_name: Chiu Man On Paul t/a Pacific Power Engineering Co. v Vaford Contracting Co. Ltd.
nodes.short_name: Chiu Man On Paul v Vaford Contracting
nodes.summary_en: Hong Kong implied-terms decision applying BP Refinery, objective interpretation, and a practical implied term to avoid payment deadlock.
nodes.summary_zh: 關於默示條款、客觀合同解釋及付款僵局默示條款的香港判例。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

nodes.label: Chiu Man On Paul v Vaford Contracting §8-10
nodes.public_excerpt: A term is only implied where the contract lacks business efficacy without it; if the agreement still works, implication is not justified.
nodes.text_private: A term is only implied where the contract lacks business efficacy without it; if the agreement still works, implication is not justified.

nodes.label: Implied terms and business efficacy
nodes.statement_en: A term is only implied where the contract lacks business efficacy without it; if the agreement still works, implication is not justified.
nodes.statement_zh: 只有在合同若無該條款便無法具有商業效用時，法院才會默示條款；若合同在沒有該條款時仍然有效，則不應默示。

nodes.label: B.P. Refinery (Westernport) Pty. Ltd. v Shire of Hastings [1977] 16 ALR 363
nodes.case_name: B.P. Refinery (Westernport) Pty. Ltd. v Shire of Hastings [1977] 16 ALR 363
nodes.short_name: B.P. Refinery (Westernpo v Shire of Hastings [1977]

nodes.label: Chiu Man On Paul v Vaford Contracting §9
nodes.public_excerpt: Implication must be assessed against the background facts known to both parties at the time of contracting.
nodes.text_private: Implication must be assessed against the background facts known to both parties at the time of contracting.

nodes.label: Objective background principle
nodes.statement_en: Implication must be assessed against the background facts known to both parties at the time of contracting.
nodes.statement_zh: 默示條款的認定需以訂立合同時雙方已知的所有背景事實為依據。

nodes.label: Investors Compensation Scheme Ltd. v West Bromwich Building Society [1998] 1 WLR 896
nodes.case_name: Investors Compensation Scheme Ltd. v West Bromwich Building Society [1998] 1 WLR 896
nodes.short_name: Investors Compensation S v West Bromwich Building S

nodes.label: Chiu Man On Paul v Vaford Contracting §17-18
nodes.public_excerpt: Where the contract omits the trigger for payment, the court may imply a reasonable-time mechanism to keep the bargain workable.
nodes.text_private: Where the contract omits the trigger for payment, the court may imply a reasonable-time mechanism to keep the bargain workable.

nodes.label: Payment deadlock implication
nodes.statement_en: Where the contract omits the trigger for payment, the court may imply a reasonable-time mechanism to keep the bargain workable.
nodes.statement_zh: 當合同未約定付款觸發條件時，法院可默示合理時間機制，以避免合同在付款問題上無法執行。

nodes.label: To Yung Sing Herman v Szeto Chak Mei and Others
nodes.case_name: To Yung Sing Herman v Szeto Chak Mei and Others
nodes.short_name: To Yung Sing Herman v Szeto Chak Mei
nodes.summary_en: Land-sale decision on vacant possession, title proof under Cap. 219, and holistic contractual interpretation.
nodes.summary_zh: 關於土地買賣合同、空置管有權及整體性合同解釋的香港判例。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

nodes.label: Special Contracts
nodes.summary: Synthetic topic created from case enrichment data for Special Contracts.

nodes.label: To Yung Sing Herman v Szeto Chak Mei §8
nodes.public_excerpt: A vendor must deliver vacant possession and prove good title under sections 13 and 13A of the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance.
nodes.text_private: A vendor must deliver vacant possession and prove good title under sections 13 and 13A of the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance.

nodes.label: Vacant possession and good title
nodes.label_zh: 空置管有權與良好所有權
nodes.statement_en: A vendor must deliver vacant possession and prove good title under sections 13 and 13A of the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance.
nodes.statement_zh: 賣方須在交房時交付空置管有權，並按《物業轉易及財產條例》第13及13A條證明良好所有權。

nodes.label: To Yung Sing Herman v Szeto Chak Mei §10-12
nodes.public_excerpt: If substantial debris or objects prevent normal use of the premises, vacant possession has not been delivered.
nodes.text_private: If substantial debris or objects prevent normal use of the premises, vacant possession has not been delivered.

nodes.label: Objective test for vacant possession
nodes.label_zh: 空置管有權的客觀認定
nodes.statement_en: If substantial debris or objects prevent normal use of the premises, vacant possession has not been delivered.
nodes.statement_zh: 若單元內遺留大量雜物或垃圾，導致無法正常使用，即構成未交付空置管有權。

nodes.label: To Yung Sing Herman v Szeto Chak Mei §36
nodes.public_excerpt: Contractual clauses should be read as a whole rather than in isolation.
nodes.text_private: Contractual clauses should be read as a whole rather than in isolation.

nodes.label: Holistic interpretation
nodes.label_zh: 合同解釋的整體性原則
nodes.statement_en: Contractual clauses should be read as a whole rather than in isolation.
nodes.statement_zh: 合同條款應作整體解釋，不能孤立看待單一條款。

nodes.label: Siemsgluss & Sohn v Yiu Shui Ching Anita t/a Everise Trading Co.
nodes.case_name: Siemsgluss & Sohn v Yiu Shui Ching Anita t/a Everise Trading Co.
nodes.short_name: Siemsgluss & Sohn v Yiu Shui Ching Anita
nodes.court_name: High Court (now Court of First Instance)
nodes.summary_en: Variation and settlement case on rescission by agreement, evidential proof of variation, and forbearance as good consideration.
nodes.summary_zh: 關於協議解除、合同變更證明及忍讓訴訟作為有效約因的香港判例。

nodes.label: Termination by Agreement
nodes.summary: Synthetic topic created from case enrichment data for Termination by Agreement.

nodes.label: Siemsgluss & Sohn v Yiu Shui Ching Anita §20-21
nodes.public_excerpt: The parties may discharge the original contract by agreeing on a fixed compensation arrangement that stands as a new bargain.
nodes.text_private: The parties may discharge the original contract by agreeing on a fixed compensation arrangement that stands as a new bargain.

nodes.label: Independent rescission bargain
nodes.statement_en: The parties may discharge the original contract by agreeing on a fixed compensation arrangement that stands as a new bargain.
nodes.statement_zh: 雙方可協議以支付固定賠償金的方式解除原合同，該安排可構成獨立的新約定。

nodes.label: Siemsgluss & Sohn v Yiu Shui Ching Anita §26
nodes.public_excerpt: A party alleging a later variation must prove clear agreement by oral or written evidence.
nodes.text_private: A party alleging a later variation must prove clear agreement by oral or written evidence.

nodes.label: Proof of contractual variation
nodes.statement_en: A party alleging a later variation must prove clear agreement by oral or written evidence.
nodes.statement_zh: 若一方主張合同已被新協議變更，須有明確的書面或口頭證據證明已達成合意。

nodes.label: Siemsgluss & Sohn v Yiu Shui Ching Anita §17-18
nodes.public_excerpt: Foregoing a damages claim in exchange for a fixed payment can amount to sufficient consideration.
nodes.text_private: Foregoing a damages claim in exchange for a fixed payment can amount to sufficient consideration.

nodes.label: Forbearance as consideration
nodes.statement_en: Foregoing a damages claim in exchange for a fixed payment can amount to sufficient consideration.
nodes.statement_zh: 一方放棄追索違約損失，換取對方支付固定賠償金，可構成有效約因。

nodes.label: Chappel & Co Ltd v Nestle Co Ltd [1960]
nodes.case_name: Chappel & Co Ltd v Nestle Co Ltd [1960]
nodes.short_name: Chappel & Co Ltd v Nestle Co Ltd [1960]

nodes.label: Travelex Hong Kong Ltd v Ettore Nassetti (Asia) Ltd
nodes.case_name: Travelex Hong Kong Ltd v Ettore Nassetti (Asia) Ltd
nodes.short_name: Travelex Hong Kong Ltd v Ettore Nassetti
nodes.summary_en: Misrepresentation case stressing proof, the weight of recordings, and the binding force of signed standard terms.
nodes.summary_zh: 關於失實陳述抗辯、錄音證據及標準格式合同效力的香港判例。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

nodes.label: Travelex Hong Kong Ltd v Ettore Nassetti §10-11, §21
nodes.public_excerpt: A party alleging inducement by oral statements must provide clear and credible evidence; recordings are especially weighty.
nodes.text_private: A party alleging inducement by oral statements must provide clear and credible evidence; recordings are especially weighty.

nodes.label: Burden of proving oral misrepresentation
nodes.label_zh: 口頭失實陳述的證明責任
nodes.statement_en: A party alleging inducement by oral statements must provide clear and credible evidence; recordings are especially weighty.
nodes.statement_zh: 主張被對方口頭陳述誤導而訂立合同的一方，須提供清晰可信的證據；錄音尤其重要。

nodes.label: Travelex Hong Kong Ltd v Ettore Nassetti §23-25
nodes.public_excerpt: Where negotiations were recorded, the recording outweighs the parties' later recollections.
nodes.text_private: Where negotiations were recorded, the recording outweighs the parties' later recollections.

nodes.label: Primacy of recordings
nodes.statement_en: Where negotiations were recorded, the recording outweighs the parties' later recollections.
nodes.statement_zh: 當關鍵口頭交涉內容被錄音時，錄音記錄優先於當事人的事後記憶。

nodes.label: Travelex Hong Kong Ltd v Ettore Nassetti §1, §26
nodes.public_excerpt: Signed standard-form trading terms bind the parties absent fraud or actionable misrepresentation.
nodes.text_private: Signed standard-form trading terms bind the parties absent fraud or actionable misrepresentation.

nodes.label: Binding force of standard-form terms
nodes.label_zh: 標準格式合同的約束力
nodes.statement_en: Signed standard-form trading terms bind the parties absent fraud or actionable misrepresentation.
nodes.statement_zh: 在商業交易中，雙方簽署的標準格式合同條款對雙方具有約束力，除非存在欺詐或失實陳述。

nodes.label: Re Hsin Chong Construction Co., Ltd.
nodes.case_name: Re Hsin Chong Construction Co., Ltd.
nodes.summary_en: CFA winding-up decision on the strict operation of section 182, the meaning of company property, and validation-order standards.
nodes.summary_zh: 終審法院就《公司（清盤及雜項條文）條例》第182條、公司財產及驗證令標準作出的權威判決。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

nodes.label: Cross-Cutting Issues
nodes.summary: Synthetic topic created from case enrichment data for Cross-Cutting Issues.

nodes.label: Re Hsin Chong Construction
nodes.public_excerpt: After the commencement of winding up, any disposition of company property is void unless the court otherwise orders.
nodes.text_private: After the commencement of winding up, any disposition of company property is void unless the court otherwise orders.

nodes.label: Strict effect of section 182
nodes.statement_en: After the commencement of winding up, any disposition of company property is void unless the court otherwise orders.
nodes.statement_zh: 清盤開始後對公司財產的任何處置，除非法院另有命令，否則無效。

nodes.label: Companies (Winding Up and Miscellaneous Provisions) Ordinance (Cap 32) s.182
nodes.title: Companies (Winding Up and Miscellaneous Provisions) Ordinance (Cap 32) s.182

nodes.label: Re Hsin Chong Construction
nodes.public_excerpt: Rights under a contract remain company property even after they are converted into a payment claim.
nodes.text_private: Rights under a contract remain company property even after they are converted into a payment claim.

nodes.label: Contractual rights as property
nodes.statement_en: Rights under a contract remain company property even after they are converted into a payment claim.
nodes.statement_zh: 合同項下的權利屬於公司財產，即使其後被轉化為付款請求權亦然。

nodes.label: Re Hsin Chong Construction
nodes.public_excerpt: A validation order should only be granted where the disposition benefits the body of unsecured creditors as a whole.
nodes.text_private: A validation order should only be granted where the disposition benefits the body of unsecured creditors as a whole.

nodes.label: Validation-order standard
nodes.statement_en: A validation order should only be granted where the disposition benefits the body of unsecured creditors as a whole.
nodes.statement_zh: 只有在處置行為對全體無擔保債權人有利時，法院才會批准驗證令。

nodes.label: MG Charter Ltd v Beijing Caissa International Travel Service Co., Ltd
nodes.case_name: MG Charter Ltd v Beijing Caissa International Travel Service Co., Ltd
nodes.short_name: MG Charter Ltd v Beijing Caissa
nodes.summary_en: Court of Appeal decision distinguishing formation and performance conditions precedent and reaffirming holistic commercial interpretation.
nodes.summary_zh: 上訴法院關於合同成立條件先例、履行條件先例及商業合理性解釋的判例。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

nodes.label: MG Charter Ltd v Beijing Caissa §36-38
nodes.public_excerpt: A condition precedent to formation determines whether a contract exists; a condition precedent to performance suspends obligations under an already-formed contract.
nodes.text_private: A condition precedent to formation determines whether a contract exists; a condition precedent to performance suspends obligations under an already-formed contract.

nodes.label: Formation versus performance conditions precedent
nodes.label_zh: 成立與履行條件先例的區分
nodes.statement_en: A condition precedent to formation determines whether a contract exists; a condition precedent to performance suspends obligations under an already-formed contract.
nodes.statement_zh: 合同成立的條件先例決定合同是否成立；履行的條件先例則是在合同已成立後暫緩履行義務。

nodes.label: MG Charter Ltd v Beijing Caissa §43-46
nodes.public_excerpt: Interpretation requires reading all contractual provisions together rather than isolating one clause.
nodes.text_private: Interpretation requires reading all contractual provisions together rather than isolating one clause.

nodes.label: Whole-contract interpretation
nodes.statement_en: Interpretation requires reading all contractual provisions together rather than isolating one clause.
nodes.statement_zh: 解釋合同不能孤立看待某一條款，必須將所有條款作為一個整體考慮。

nodes.label: MG Charter Ltd v Beijing Caissa §46-47, §49
nodes.public_excerpt: The court should resist commercially unreasonable readings and may still use background facts even where an entire-agreement clause exists.
nodes.text_private: The court should resist commercially unreasonable readings and may still use background facts even where an entire-agreement clause exists.

nodes.label: Commercial reasonableness and background facts
nodes.label_zh: 商業合理性與背景事實
nodes.statement_en: The court should resist commercially unreasonable readings and may still use background facts even where an entire-agreement clause exists.
nodes.statement_zh: 解釋合約時應避免導致商業上不合理的結果；即使有完整合約條款，仍可援引背景事實協助解釋。

nodes.label: Wong Yee Chuk v Lo Po Wai, Harry and Others
nodes.case_name: Wong Yee Chuk v Lo Po Wai, Harry and Others
nodes.short_name: Wong Yee Chuk v Lo Po Wai
nodes.summary_en: Settlement-formation case reaffirming mirror acceptance and the counter-offer rule in written compromise negotiations.
nodes.summary_zh: 關於和解協議成立及反要約原則的香港判例。

nodes.label: Wong Yee Chuk v Lo Po Wai §6-8
nodes.public_excerpt: A binding settlement is formed when one side offers clear written terms and the other accepts them in writing without introducing new conditions.
nodes.text_private: A binding settlement is formed when one side offers clear written terms and the other accepts them in writing without introducing new conditions.

nodes.label: Formation of a settlement agreement
nodes.statement_en: A binding settlement is formed when one side offers clear written terms and the other accepts them in writing without introducing new conditions.
nodes.statement_zh: 一方以書面提出明確和解條款，另一方以書面接受且未加入新條件時，即成立有約束力的和解協議。

nodes.label: Wong Yee Chuk v Lo Po Wai §8
nodes.public_excerpt: Acceptance must mirror the offer, with no less and no more; additions or alterations amount to a counter-offer.
nodes.text_private: Acceptance must mirror the offer, with no less and no more; additions or alterations amount to a counter-offer.

nodes.label: Mirror acceptance
nodes.statement_en: Acceptance must mirror the offer, with no less and no more; additions or alterations amount to a counter-offer.
nodes.statement_zh: 接受必須與要約條款完全一致，無增無減；任何添加或修改都構成反要約。

nodes.label: Eton Properties Ltd and Eton Properties Holdings Ltd v Xiamen Xinjingdi Group Co., Ltd
nodes.case_name: Eton Properties Ltd and Eton Properties Holdings Ltd v Xiamen Xinjingdi Group Co., Ltd
nodes.summary_en: Judgment on mala fide repeat proceedings, issue estoppel, and the Henderson v Henderson abuse principle.
nodes.summary_zh: 關於惡意重複程序、既判力及 Henderson v Henderson 原則的香港判例。

nodes.label: Eton Properties v Xiamen Xinjingdi §9-11
nodes.public_excerpt: Launching new proceedings in another forum after final Hong Kong determinations can be mala fide where it reopens matters already decided.
nodes.text_private: Launching new proceedings in another forum after final Hong Kong determinations can be mala fide where it reopens matters already decided.

nodes.label: Mala fide repeat proceedings
nodes.statement_en: Launching new proceedings in another forum after final Hong Kong determinations can be mala fide where it reopens matters already decided.
nodes.statement_zh: 在香港已有終局判決後，若一方在其他法域啟動新程序以重新爭議既決事項，可構成惡意程序。

nodes.label: Eton Properties v Xiamen Xinjingdi §12-19
nodes.public_excerpt: Issues resolved by arbitral awards or court judgments should not be re-litigated.
nodes.text_private: Issues resolved by arbitral awards or court judgments should not be re-litigated.

nodes.label: Res judicata and issue estoppel
nodes.statement_en: Issues resolved by arbitral awards or court judgments should not be re-litigated.
nodes.statement_zh: 仲裁裁決和法院判決已確定的事項，不得重複訴訟。

metadata.case_name: Chiu Man On Paul t/a Pacific Power Engineering Co. v Vaford Contracting Co. Ltd.
metadata.short_name: Chiu Man On Paul v Vaford Contracting
metadata.summary_en: Hong Kong implied-terms decision applying BP Refinery, objective interpretation, and a practical implied term to avoid payment deadlock.
metadata.summary_zh: 關於默示條款、客觀合同解釋及付款僵局默示條款的香港判例。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

principles.statement_en: A term is only implied where the contract lacks business efficacy without it; if the agreement still works, implication is not justified.
principles.statement_zh: 只有在合同若無該條款便無法具有商業效用時，法院才會默示條款；若合同在沒有該條款時仍然有效，則不應默示。
principles.public_excerpt: A term is only implied where the contract lacks business efficacy without it; if the agreement still works, implication is not justified.
principles.text_private: A term is only implied where the contract lacks business efficacy without it; if the agreement still works, implication is not justified.

cited_authority.label: B.P. Refinery (Westernport) Pty. Ltd. v Shire of Hastings [1977] 16 ALR 363

principles.statement_en: Implication must be assessed against the background facts known to both parties at the time of contracting.
principles.statement_zh: 默示條款的認定需以訂立合同時雙方已知的所有背景事實為依據。
principles.public_excerpt: Implication must be assessed against the background facts known to both parties at the time of contracting.
principles.text_private: Implication must be assessed against the background facts known to both parties at the time of contracting.

cited_authority.label: Investors Compensation Scheme Ltd. v West Bromwich Building Society [1998] 1 WLR 896

principles.statement_en: Where the contract omits the trigger for payment, the court may imply a reasonable-time mechanism to keep the bargain workable.
principles.statement_zh: 當合同未約定付款觸發條件時，法院可默示合理時間機制，以避免合同在付款問題上無法執行。
principles.public_excerpt: Where the contract omits the trigger for payment, the court may imply a reasonable-time mechanism to keep the bargain workable.
principles.text_private: Where the contract omits the trigger for payment, the court may imply a reasonable-time mechanism to keep the bargain workable.

relationships.target_label: Investors Compensation Scheme Ltd. v West Bromwich Building Society [1998] 1 WLR 896
relationships.explanation: Applies the objective interpretation principle to implication analysis.

metadata.case_name: To Yung Sing Herman v Szeto Chak Mei and Others
metadata.short_name: To Yung Sing Herman v Szeto Chak Mei
metadata.summary_en: Land-sale decision on vacant possession, title proof under Cap. 219, and holistic contractual interpretation.
metadata.summary_zh: 關於土地買賣合同、空置管有權及整體性合同解釋的香港判例。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

principles.label_zh: 空置管有權與良好所有權
principles.statement_en: A vendor must deliver vacant possession and prove good title under sections 13 and 13A of the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance.
principles.statement_zh: 賣方須在交房時交付空置管有權，並按《物業轉易及財產條例》第13及13A條證明良好所有權。
principles.public_excerpt: A vendor must deliver vacant possession and prove good title under sections 13 and 13A of the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance.
principles.text_private: A vendor must deliver vacant possession and prove good title under sections 13 and 13A of the Conveyancing and Property Ordinance.

principles.label_zh: 空置管有權的客觀認定
principles.statement_en: If substantial debris or objects prevent normal use of the premises, vacant possession has not been delivered.
principles.statement_zh: 若單元內遺留大量雜物或垃圾，導致無法正常使用，即構成未交付空置管有權。
principles.public_excerpt: If substantial debris or objects prevent normal use of the premises, vacant possession has not been delivered.
principles.text_private: If substantial debris or objects prevent normal use of the premises, vacant possession has not been delivered.

principles.label_zh: 合同解釋的整體性原則
principles.statement_en: Contractual clauses should be read as a whole rather than in isolation.
principles.statement_zh: 合同條款應作整體解釋，不能孤立看待單一條款。
principles.public_excerpt: Contractual clauses should be read as a whole rather than in isolation.
principles.text_private: Contractual clauses should be read as a whole rather than in isolation.

relationships.target_label: Conveyancing and Property Ordinance (Cap 219) ss. 13, 13A
relationships.explanation: Explains the vendor's title-proof obligations in a land-sale contract.

statutory_interpretations.explanation: Explains the vendor's title-proof obligations in a land-sale contract.

metadata.case_name: Siemsgluss & Sohn v Yiu Shui Ching Anita t/a Everise Trading Co.
metadata.short_name: Siemsgluss & Sohn v Yiu Shui Ching Anita
metadata.court_name: High Court (now Court of First Instance)
metadata.summary_en: Variation and settlement case on rescission by agreement, evidential proof of variation, and forbearance as good consideration.
metadata.summary_zh: 關於協議解除、合同變更證明及忍讓訴訟作為有效約因的香港判例。

principles.statement_en: Foregoing a damages claim in exchange for a fixed payment can amount to sufficient consideration.
principles.statement_zh: 一方放棄追索違約損失，換取對方支付固定賠償金，可構成有效約因。
principles.public_excerpt: Foregoing a damages claim in exchange for a fixed payment can amount to sufficient consideration.
principles.text_private: Foregoing a damages claim in exchange for a fixed payment can amount to sufficient consideration.

cited_authority.label: Chappel & Co Ltd v Nestle Co Ltd [1960]

principles.statement_en: The parties may discharge the original contract by agreeing on a fixed compensation arrangement that stands as a new bargain.
principles.statement_zh: 雙方可協議以支付固定賠償金的方式解除原合同，該安排可構成獨立的新約定。
principles.public_excerpt: The parties may discharge the original contract by agreeing on a fixed compensation arrangement that stands as a new bargain.
principles.text_private: The parties may discharge the original contract by agreeing on a fixed compensation arrangement that stands as a new bargain.

principles.statement_en: A party alleging a later variation must prove clear agreement by oral or written evidence.
principles.statement_zh: 若一方主張合同已被新協議變更，須有明確的書面或口頭證據證明已達成合意。
principles.public_excerpt: A party alleging a later variation must prove clear agreement by oral or written evidence.
principles.text_private: A party alleging a later variation must prove clear agreement by oral or written evidence.

relationships.target_label: Chappel & Co Ltd v Nestle Co Ltd [1960]
relationships.explanation: Uses the sufficiency-not-adequacy approach to consideration.

relationships.target_label: Chappel & Co Ltd v Nestle Co Ltd [1960]

metadata.case_name: Travelex Hong Kong Ltd v Ettore Nassetti (Asia) Ltd
metadata.short_name: Travelex Hong Kong Ltd v Ettore Nassetti
metadata.summary_en: Misrepresentation case stressing proof, the weight of recordings, and the binding force of signed standard terms.
metadata.summary_zh: 關於失實陳述抗辯、錄音證據及標準格式合同效力的香港判例。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

principles.label_zh: 標準格式合同的約束力
principles.statement_en: Signed standard-form trading terms bind the parties absent fraud or actionable misrepresentation.
principles.statement_zh: 在商業交易中，雙方簽署的標準格式合同條款對雙方具有約束力，除非存在欺詐或失實陳述。
principles.public_excerpt: Signed standard-form trading terms bind the parties absent fraud or actionable misrepresentation.
principles.text_private: Signed standard-form trading terms bind the parties absent fraud or actionable misrepresentation.

principles.label_zh: 口頭失實陳述的證明責任
principles.statement_en: A party alleging inducement by oral statements must provide clear and credible evidence; recordings are especially weighty.
principles.statement_zh: 主張被對方口頭陳述誤導而訂立合同的一方，須提供清晰可信的證據；錄音尤其重要。
principles.public_excerpt: A party alleging inducement by oral statements must provide clear and credible evidence; recordings are especially weighty.
principles.text_private: A party alleging inducement by oral statements must provide clear and credible evidence; recordings are especially weighty.

principles.statement_en: Where negotiations were recorded, the recording outweighs the parties' later recollections.
principles.statement_zh: 當關鍵口頭交涉內容被錄音時，錄音記錄優先於當事人的事後記憶。
principles.public_excerpt: Where negotiations were recorded, the recording outweighs the parties' later recollections.
principles.text_private: Where negotiations were recorded, the recording outweighs the parties' later recollections.

relationships.target_label: Derry v Peek (1889)
relationships.explanation: Applies the classic fraud standard when assessing the pleaded misrepresentation case.

relationships.target_label: Derry v Peek (1889)
relationships.explanation: principle citation

metadata.case_name: Re Hsin Chong Construction Co., Ltd.
metadata.summary_en: CFA winding-up decision on the strict operation of section 182, the meaning of company property, and validation-order standards.
metadata.summary_zh: 終審法院就《公司（清盤及雜項條文）條例》第182條、公司財產及驗證令標準作出的權威判決。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

principles.statement_en: After the commencement of winding up, any disposition of company property is void unless the court otherwise orders.
principles.statement_zh: 清盤開始後對公司財產的任何處置，除非法院另有命令，否則無效。
principles.public_excerpt: After the commencement of winding up, any disposition of company property is void unless the court otherwise orders.
principles.text_private: After the commencement of winding up, any disposition of company property is void unless the court otherwise orders.

cited_authority.label: Companies (Winding Up and Miscellaneous Provisions) Ordinance (Cap 32) s.182

principles.statement_en: Rights under a contract remain company property even after they are converted into a payment claim.
principles.statement_zh: 合同項下的權利屬於公司財產，即使其後被轉化為付款請求權亦然。
principles.public_excerpt: Rights under a contract remain company property even after they are converted into a payment claim.
principles.text_private: Rights under a contract remain company property even after they are converted into a payment claim.

principles.statement_en: A validation order should only be granted where the disposition benefits the body of unsecured creditors as a whole.
principles.statement_zh: 只有在處置行為對全體無擔保債權人有利時，法院才會批准驗證令。
principles.public_excerpt: A validation order should only be granted where the disposition benefits the body of unsecured creditors as a whole.
principles.text_private: A validation order should only be granted where the disposition benefits the body of unsecured creditors as a whole.

relationships.target_label: Companies (Winding Up and Miscellaneous Provisions) Ordinance (Cap 32) s.182
relationships.explanation: Provides the CFA's authoritative construction of section 182.

statutory_interpretations.target_label: Companies (Winding Up and Miscellaneous Provisions) Ordinance (Cap 32) s.182
statutory_interpretations.explanation: Provides the CFA's authoritative construction of section 182.

metadata.case_name: MG Charter Ltd v Beijing Caissa International Travel Service Co., Ltd
metadata.short_name: MG Charter Ltd v Beijing Caissa
metadata.summary_en: Court of Appeal decision distinguishing formation and performance conditions precedent and reaffirming holistic commercial interpretation.
metadata.summary_zh: 上訴法院關於合同成立條件先例、履行條件先例及商業合理性解釋的判例。
topic_paths: Contents/Term Identification/Express Terms and the Parol Evidence Rule

principles.label_zh: 成立與履行條件先例的區分
principles.statement_en: A condition precedent to formation determines whether a contract exists; a condition precedent to performance suspends obligations under an already-formed contract.
principles.statement_zh: 合同成立的條件先例決定合同是否成立；履行的條件先例則是在合同已成立後暫緩履行義務。
principles.public_excerpt: A condition precedent to formation determines whether a contract exists; a condition precedent to performance suspends obligations under an already-formed contract.
principles.text_private: A condition precedent to formation determines whether a contract exists; a condition precedent to performance suspends obligations under an already-formed contract.

principles.statement_en: Interpretation requires reading all contractual provisions together rather than isolating one clause.
principles.statement_zh: 解釋合同不能孤立看待某一條款，必須將所有條款作為一個整體考慮。
principles.public_excerpt: Interpretation requires reading all contractual provisions together rather than isolating one clause.
principles.text_private: Interpretation requires reading all contractual provisions together rather than isolating one clause.

principles.label_zh: 商業合理性與背景事實
principles.statement_en: The court should resist commercially unreasonable readings and may still use background facts even where an entire-agreement clause exists.
principles.statement_zh: 解釋合約時應避免導致商業上不合理的結果；即使有完整合約條款，仍可援引背景事實協助解釋。
principles.public_excerpt: The court should resist commercially unreasonable readings and may still use background facts even where an entire-agreement clause exists.
principles.text_private: The court should resist commercially unreasonable readings and may still use background facts even where an entire-agreement clause exists.

cited_authority.label: Investors Compensation Scheme Ltd. v West Bromwich Building Society [1998] 1 WLR 896

relationships.target_label: Investors Compensation Scheme Ltd. v West Bromwich Building Society [1998] 1 WLR 896
relationships.explanation: Applies the background-facts principle in contractual interpretation.

metadata.case_name: Wong Yee Chuk v Lo Po Wai, Harry and Others
metadata.short_name: Wong Yee Chuk v Lo Po Wai
metadata.summary_en: Settlement-formation case reaffirming mirror acceptance and the counter-offer rule in written compromise negotiations.
metadata.summary_zh: 關於和解協議成立及反要約原則的香港判例。

principles.statement_en: A binding settlement is formed when one side offers clear written terms and the other accepts them in writing without introducing new conditions.
principles.statement_zh: 一方以書面提出明確和解條款，另一方以書面接受且未加入新條件時，即成立有約束力的和解協議。
principles.public_excerpt: A binding settlement is formed when one side offers clear written terms and the other accepts them in writing without introducing new conditions.
principles.text_private: A binding settlement is formed when one side offers clear written terms and the other accepts them in writing without introducing new conditions.

principles.statement_en: Acceptance must mirror the offer, with no less and no more; additions or alterations amount to a counter-offer.
principles.statement_zh: 接受必須與要約條款完全一致，無增無減；任何添加或修改都構成反要約。
principles.public_excerpt: Acceptance must mirror the offer, with no less and no more; additions or alterations amount to a counter-offer.
principles.text_private: Acceptance must mirror the offer, with no less and no more; additions or alterations amount to a counter-offer.

relationships.target_label: Hyde v Wrench (1840)
relationships.explanation: Applies the classic counter-offer principle to settlement correspondence.

relationships.target_label: Hyde v Wrench (1840)
relationships.explanation: principle citation

metadata.case_name: Eton Properties Ltd and Eton Properties Holdings Ltd v Xiamen Xinjingdi Group Co., Ltd
metadata.summary_en: Judgment on mala fide repeat proceedings, issue estoppel, and the Henderson v Henderson abuse principle.
metadata.summary_zh: 關於惡意重複程序、既判力及 Henderson v Henderson 原則的香港判例。

principles.statement_en: Launching new proceedings in another forum after final Hong Kong determinations can be mala fide where it reopens matters already decided.
principles.statement_zh: 在香港已有終局判決後，若一方在其他法域啟動新程序以重新爭議既決事項，可構成惡意程序。
principles.public_excerpt: Launching new proceedings in another forum after final Hong Kong determinations can be mala fide where it reopens matters already decided.
principles.text_private: Launching new proceedings in another forum after final Hong Kong determinations can be mala fide where it reopens matters already decided.

principles.statement_en: Issues resolved by arbitral awards or court judgments should not be re-litigated.
principles.statement_zh: 仲裁裁決和法院判決已確定的事項，不得重複訴訟。
principles.public_excerpt: Issues resolved by arbitral awards or court judgments should not be re-litigated.
principles.text_private: Issues resolved by arbitral awards or court judgments should not be re-litigated.

relationships.target_label: Henderson v Henderson (1843)
relationships.explanation: Applies the one-stop litigation principle to repeat proceedings.
